2605.16865.title: MixSD: Mixed Contextual Self-Distillation for Knowledge Injection
2605.16865.paper_summary: Supervised fine-tuning (SFT) is widely used to inject new knowledge into language models, but it often degrades pretrained capabilities such as reasoning and general-domain performance. We argue this forgetting arises because fine-tuning targets from humans or external systems diverge from the model's autoregressive distribution, forcing the optimizer to imitate low-probability token sequences. To address this problem, we propose MixSD, a simple external-teacher-free method for distribution-aligned knowledge injection. Instead of training on fixed targets, MixSD constructs supervision dynamically by mixing tokens from two conditionals of the base model itself: an expert conditional that observes the injected fact in context, and a naive conditional that reflects the model's original prior. The resulting supervision sequences preserve the factual learning signal while remaining substantially closer to the base model's distribution. We evaluate MixSD on two synthetic corpora that we construct to study factual recall and arithmetic function acquisition in a controlled setting, together with established benchmarks for open-domain factual question answering and knowledge editing. Across multiple model scales and settings, MixSD consistently achieves a better memorization-retention trade-off compared to SFT and on-policy self distillation baselines, retaining up to 100% of the base model's held-out capability while maintaining near-perfect training accuracy, whereas standard SFT retains as little as 1%. We further show that MixSD produces substantially lower-NLL supervision targets under the base model and reduces harmful movement along Fisher-sensitive parameter directions. These results suggest that aligning supervision with the model's native generation distribution is a simple and effective principle for knowledge injection that mitigates catastrophic forgetting.
2605.16865.paper_summary_zh: 監督微調（SFT）被廣泛用於將新知識注入語言模型中，但它往往會降低預訓練能力，例如推理和一般領域的表現。我們認為這種遺忘的原因在於來自人類或外部系統的微調目標與模型的自回歸分佈發生偏離，迫使優化器模仿低概率的標記序列。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了MixSD，這是一種簡單的無外部教師的方法，用於分佈對齊的知識注入。MixSD不是在固定目標上進行訓練，而是通過混合來自基礎模型自身的兩個條件的標記動態構建監督：一個專家條件觀察上下文中的注入事實，另一個天真的條件反映模型的原始先驗。生成的監督序列保留了事實學習信號，同時在實質上更接近基礎模型的分佈。我們在兩個合成語料庫上評估MixSD，這些語料庫是我們構建的，用於在受控環境中研究事實回憶和算術功能獲取，並結合了開放領域事實問答和知識編輯的既定基準。在多個模型規模和設置中，MixSD始終實現了比SFT和政策內自蒸餾基準更好的記憶保持權衡，保留了基礎模型高達100%的保留能力，同時保持近乎完美的訓練準確性，而標準SFT僅保留1%。我們進一步表明，MixSD在基礎模型下產生了實質上較低的NLL監督目標，並減少了沿著Fisher敏感參數方向的有害移動。這些結果表明，將監督與模型的本地生成分佈對齊是一個簡單而有效的知識注入原則，可以減輕災難性遺忘。
2605.16865.author: Jiarui Liu et.al.
2605.16865.authors: Jiarui Liu, Lechen Zhang, Yongjin Yang, Yinghui He, Yingheng Wang, Weihao Xuan, Zhijing Jin, Mona Diab
